name: DeepSeek-R1-0528
archDisplay: DeepSeekMoE: 256 routed + 1 shared expert, top-8 routing, 61 layers (3 dense + 58 MoE), Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA)
note: Major R1 refresh with deeper chain-of-thought (AIME24 79.8 to 91.4), fewer hallucinations, and added JSON/function calling; approached o3/Gemini 2.5 Pro.
tpsNote: 671B MoE far exceeds a single 24GB 4090; Unsloth's dynamic quants shrink it to ~185GB for CPU-offload rigs, still requiring far more than one consumer GPU.

results.notes: Codeforces-Div1 rating; DeepSeek changed Codeforces methodology between R1 and R1-0528 (the original R1 card reports a differently-defined 'Codeforces (Rating): 2029' overall metric, not Div1-specific, so these are not directly comparable across the two model cards)
ARC-AGI-1 Semi Private Eval, independently run by ARC Prize (not on DeepSeek's own model card). Cost $0.046/task; ARC Prize noted this puts R1-0528 on par with OpenAI o4-mini (low).
ARC-AGI-2 Semi Private Eval, independently run by ARC Prize (not on DeepSeek's own model card). Cost $0.052/task.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via HF model card + DeepSeek API docs news post. 671B total / 37B active cross-verified via OpenRouter/independent sources (an AI-summarized fetch briefly mis-stated 685B; rejected as summarizer error, primary 671B/37B figure holds). MIT license, 2025-05-28 date, and all 6 benchmark rows (AIME 91.4, GPQA 81.0, LiveCodeBench 73.3, SWE-bench 57.6, HLE 17.7, MMLU-Pro 85.0) confirmed verbatim on the HF model card. Note's '79.8 to 91.4' AIME progression confirmed against original R1 (Jan 2025) score of 79.8. Price $0.55/$2.19 confirmed as DeepSeek's official standard rate for R1-0528.

2026-07-16: Checked all 4 missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, mmmu) via (1) the official HF model card benchmark table (fetched live: General/Code/Math/Tools categories = MMLU-Redux, MMLU-Pro, GPQA-Diamond, SimpleQA, FRAMES, HLE, LiveCodeBench, Codeforces-Div1, SWE-Verified, Aider-Polyglot, AIME24, AIME25, HMMT25, CNMO24, BFCL_v3, Tau-Bench — no plain MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, or HumanEval row), (2) Epoch AI's deepseek-r1-0528 model page and independent-benchmarking-hub search, and Vals.ai's models list (no DeepSeek R1-0528 entry at all on Vals.ai as of this date), and (3) cross-referenced the sibling deepseek-r1-671b.json (original Jan-2025 R1) which DOES carry self-reported mmlu=90.8 and math=97.3 from its own HF card — confirming DeepSeek deliberately dropped plain MMLU and MATH from the R1-0528 refresh in favor of MMLU-Redux/MMLU-Pro and AIME/HMMT/CNMO respectively, rather than the numbers never having existed for the family. No credible primary or independent-tracker source publishes mmlu, math, or humaneval for R1-0528 specifically; only low-authority aggregator sites (e.g. llm-stats.com, benchgen.com, chat-deep.ai) show numbers for these slugs, and direct WebFetch of llm-stats.com and artificialanalysis.ai confirmed those sites do NOT actually surface sourced MMLU/MATH/HumanEval scores for this model despite appearing in search snippets — treated as unreliable/unverifiable and not used. mmmu is not applicable: R1-0528 is confirmed text-only (capabilities.vision=false, modalities=[\"text\"]), so it was never evaluated on this vision-language benchmark. All four gaps resolved as omitted_no_evidence; no existing results were altered.

2026-07-18: Re-checked the mmmu gap for this model per round-22 research pass. Confirmed directly from this model's own corpus record that DeepSeek-R1-0528 is text-only: modalities=["text"] and capabilities.vision=false. DeepSeek's multimodal capability lives in a separate model line (DeepSeek-VL2, Janus-Pro), not in the R1 reasoning line — R1-0528 has no vision encoder/input path. Re-inspected the official HF model card benchmark table (General/Code/Math/Tools categories: MMLU-Redux, MMLU-Pro, GPQA-Diamond, SimpleQA, FRAMES, HLE, LiveCodeBench, Codeforces-Div1, SWE-Verified, Aider-Polyglot, AIME24/25, HMMT25, CNMO24, BFCL_v3, Tau-Bench) and confirmed it contains no vision-language section at all, consistent with a text-only architecture (this corroborates rather than duplicates the 2026-07-16 finding already on file, which reached the same conclusion). MMMU is architecturally inapplicable to DeepSeek-R1-0528, not merely an unpublished/not-yet-found number — no vision-language eval was ever run because the model has no vision input capability. No new result added; existing results[] left completely unchanged.
